Hadley told her mother that Tristan wanted to bring her to Romania and take her to visit waterfalls and cultural festivals. “很棒的计划！让我梦想成真，”她的母亲回答道。 “但要牢牢地固定好你的脚。” Tristan texted Hadley that she'd live in a big house, close to his own.她会教他钢琴，他会帮助她开启音乐生涯。在支付了她的机票费用后，他制定了一些基本规则。首先是他不能容忍体毛。但最重要的是，除非他介绍她，否则她绝不能与罗马尼亚的任何人交谈。 “罗马尼亚是我的世界，”他写道。 “No friends from outside.”

When Hadley landed in Bucharest, Tristan picked her up in a blue Rolls-Royce.她回忆说，在开车前往大院的路上，他开玩笑说要绑架她，这让她感到不安。 When he took her into his bedroom, she later told police, “I simply froze and had sex with him.”

Hadley soon learned that Tristan was having sex with several of his workers. “我没有哭，也没有抱怨，”她说。 “我很害怕。”他敦促她开始在 OnlyFans 上为他发帖，但她拒绝了。 “I can't show my body,” she texted him. “我想教你音乐并在附近找一份工作。”尽管如此，三天后，特里斯坦还是把哈德利和其他新兵一起搬进了 67 号别墅，她认为这些新兵看起来不高兴而且营养不良。 Later, she said, she heard Naghel threatening to beat them if they broke the rules.

Hadley noticed another newcomer at the villa: a young Moldovan woman whom I'll call Liana Vintilla.她似乎总是心烦意乱，哈德利决定和她谈谈。 At a party at the compound, she found a moment to draw her aside.

Hadley learned that Vintilla had been working as a restaurant hostess in London when Andrew connected with her on Instagram and began bombarding her with flattery.当她在网上发现他谈论拉皮条的视频时，他向她保证这只是“演艺事业”。但是，当她搬到美国村时，他坚持让她在 OnlyFans 上发帖。经过几周的胁迫和监禁，她屈服了。 “Your wife goes live today,” she texted him.

Vintilla said that she was alarmed by life at the villa, where her housemates had “nightmares, spasms in their sleep.”她告诉哈德利她想逃跑，但泰特禁止她离开。她很害怕他。他通过短信告诉她，他与黑手党有联系，可能会试图找到她，而且他的血液化学成分与连环杀手相同。几天前，当她写信告诉他她很痛苦后，他把她叫到他的卧室。文蒂拉说，在那里，他打了她的脸并强奸了她，双手痛苦地挤压着她的头。她说，他威胁要让她怀孕，然后把她锁在房子里，这样她“肯定会发疯”。

That night, Hadley texted her mother, “Tristan and his brother are terrible people.” She said that she was making a plan to leave, along with “a girl who's been stuck here.” Her mother replied, “I told you. Now try to get out of there quietly.”

Hadley booked seats on a flight to London, departing in two days. She texted Vintilla that they would have to be careful with the brothers, to “make them believe we are in love” so that they would “not suspect us of leaving.” Vintilla agreed to “play it cool.” They imagined that one day they would look back on the experience and write a movie. Vintilla wrote, “We need a fucking Oscar.”

While the women prepared to flee, Hadley texted a family friend who was a marine. She said that the Tates were keeping her in a house with other women who acted as “groomers and handlers.” Her friend, who had learned to spot signs of trafficking during his military training, concluded that her life was in danger. He called his superior officer, who urged him to report the situation to the US Embassy.

When Hadley learned what he had done, she panicked. “This is super scary,” she texted him. “They will kill us.” As the raid team approached, she ran upstairs and locked herself in her bedroom—which is where they found her when they broke down the door.

Mateea Petrescu was at her grandfather's funeral outside Bucharest when her phone began buzzing with news: the Tate brothers had been accused of forming an organized-crime group to traffic women, and were being held in jail. Petrescu was a PR consultant in her late thirties who vaped continually, dressed in couture, and swore with creative flair. She knew all about the Tates; her cousin Eugen Vidineac was their local lawyer, and he'd been asking her for advice. Petrescu looked over at Vidineac, who was weeping as the casket was lowered into the ground. “Orthodox Romanian funerals are in a league of their own,” she told me. “They try to squeeze the last ounce of pain and suffering out of you.” Vidineac seemed too distraught to deal with the news, so she told him that she'd handle it.

In Bucharest, Petrescu joined the Tates' team as the spokeswoman. She was thrilled. Many of her clients were nationalist Romanian politicians; the Tates were global celebrities. “It was the case of the moment,” she told me, and everyone she knew was “in awe.”

One of her first tasks was responding to a report that the Taliban were concerned about Andrew Tate's well-being. The brothers had been trying to secure residency in Dubai, and Andrew had announced that he'd converted to Islam. When the Taliban seized control of Kabul, barring women from work and girls from school, he had tweeted approvingly, “Dinner will be ready on TIME . The Taliban restores order. Inshallah.” Now the group was apparently calling for his freedom.

Petrescu's initial counsel was “We don't want to associate with the Taliban,” but, she conceded, “we don't really want to upset the Taliban, either.” Privately, she thought, “Wow, this is my life now. I don't want to upset the Taliban.”

For Petrescu, that kind of cognitive dissonance was part of the thrill of the job. She loved being the only woman on Tate's crisis team and prided herself on being paid more than the men.当泰特在监狱里时，他无法访问互联网，但他的“泰特演讲”时事通讯和他在 X 上的帖子不断出现。佩特雷斯库告诉我，其中许多文字都是她写的。 She got a peculiar “amusement” from ventriloquizing the world's most notorious misogynist, she said.但泰特也给她留下了深刻的印象。 “I'm absolutely fascinated by the balls he has, to say the things he says and not have an ounce of a sense of ridiculousness or guilt,” she told me.

The weeks after Tate's detention were chaotic, Petrescu said.她几乎无法追踪那些渴望参与此案的律师和顾问。 “每个人都看到了生有金蛋的鹅，”她告诉我。 She had expected someone as famous as Tate to have a well-structured operation, but what she encountered seemed to run on “a long chain of impulses and the deep belief that there is some sort of godlike inspiration that cannot go wrong.”

The Tates had a deputy managing their affairs while they were in prison: their cousin Luke Leilas, who had moved from the US to work for them. On Andrew's instructions, Leilas had been directing impassioned videos in which Hruskova, Pencov, and Anghel denied all the charges. “Don't let them get lazy and wake up in the morning and look like shit,” Tate told Leilas. “No one cares what ugly girls say, they have to look amazing.”

Anghel gave an interview to Antena TV—the company where she had been a producer before Tate made her abandon her career—insisting that she was not a victim. Asked why she had Tate's name tattooed on her arm, she said, “Out of respect.”赫鲁斯科娃出现在她自己的视频中，穿着一件毛领外套，妆容完美。 “想象一下，如果这种事发生在你的儿子或兄弟身上，你会有多愤怒和愤怒，”她用英语说道。 “This is just a massive, massive violation against human rights.”

Tate was pleased. “继续前进！”他告诉莱拉斯，并补充道：“我们需要女孩们哭泣。”但他们的通讯造成了一个漏洞：检察官可以查阅监狱通话记录。 Tate's lawyers were dismayed when they found out. “I explicitly said to him that EVERYTHING IS RECORDED ,” one complained in a chat. “I told him not once, but 5 times and still.”

Nonetheless, the lawyers successfully petitioned the court to release the Tates, on the condition that they stay in Romania and check in periodically with the police.当安德鲁出狱时，他宣布：“我相信上帝，我认为真理之火最终会摧毁所有谎言。”

罗马尼亚长期以来被视为欧洲人口贩运之都，官员腐败在这一贸易中发挥着重要作用。但负责泰茨夫妇案件的检察官拉雷什·斯坦 (Rareş Stan) 名声清白。 He was known as a pugnacious investigator who had dismantled some of the country's most violent gangs.

As the brothers' assets were seized, Ferraris, McLarens, and Lamborghinis were towed through their compound's gates.调查人员在里面发现了枪支、刀具和成堆的现金。 They also seized phones and computers, containing a vast trove of digital evidence that Stan's team used to track down dozens of the brothers' recruits.

When the police interviewed Hadley, she urged them to help the other women who still supported the Tates. “这些女孩不是她们自己，”她说。 In Romania, victims in criminal cases can be eligible for damages running to hundreds of thousands of dollars, but Hadley didn't want the money. “The only things she wants are peace and quiet,” investigators noted.

Vintilla gave a similar statement, and the two women agreed to coöperate with the authorities.他们交出了手机，接受了心理评估，并提供了宣誓证词。随后，检察官安排他们被送往法国南部的一个安全屋。 Hadley posted a picture of herself wearing white linen by the sea.

In June, 2023, Stan filed an indictment of more than three hundred pages, detailing evidence that the Tates, Naghel, and Radu had trafficked the women housed in Villa 67. (The indictment was sealed, but I obtained a copy, along with an extensive cache of prosecutorial records.) The same month, prosecutors raided thirteen properties linked to Vlad Obuzic, Tate's disciple.他因涉嫌贩卖人口被捕。 Obuzic denied the charges, and is currently awaiting trial.

Meanwhile, Stan was building a wider case, involving at least thirty-five suspected victims—some of whom were minors.他确定，当泰特第一次强迫赫鲁斯科娃拍摄色情作品时，她还未成年。 He also pursued a case involving a girl I'll call Irina Pavel.

她告诉调查人员，帕维尔十五岁的时候，她陪一位同学参加了泰茨夫妇举办的聚会，聚会上全是年轻女孩。她的朋友和特里斯坦结对，把她留给了安德鲁，后者喝得酩酊大醉，说话时吐了口水。她说，他把她拉进卧室并强奸了她。 Afterward, some of Tate's “female friends” persuaded the girls to stick around for several days, and Tate kept having sex with Pavel. “我们有正常的口交和肛交，但我提到肛交行为对我来说是一种创伤，因为我很痛苦，”她告诉调查人员。 “Even though I asked Andrew to stop, he continued.”

The girls eventually had to go back to school, but, Pavel said, the Tates sent a taxi every few weeks to bring them to the compound.泰特“经常咄咄逼人”，用皮带打她，掐她的脖子，但他也给她买了泰迪熊。 Finally, he discovered that she had been chatting with a boy online and became furious, throwing her phone into the pool and kicking her out.

After Tate's arrest, Pavel said, he took her to dinner at a restaurant called Arogant and tried to make amends.他告诉她，他希望她做一个“好女孩”，不要对当局说什么，并提出支付她五千欧元，其中一半是预付的。他还给她买了一部假手机，而不是她自己的手机，送给调查人员。帕维尔拿走了电话和钱，但最终决定告诉调查人员泰特强奸了她。 She wrote in a text, “You take her so young, you fool her and make her your slave? Fuck you.”

Ella Hadley spent a while recuperating in France, then decided to keep travelling.最终，她到达了悉尼。 She was fearful of the Tates' connections, and often disguised her appearance, but she liked spending her days outside, hiking and shopping at an open-air market.

A few weeks after the brothers' arrests made the news, Hadley got a text from a former boyfriend in Florida, Marlin Fisher.他想知道带她去罗马尼亚的那个人是不是安德鲁·泰特。

“His brother,” she replied.

“Holy shit! I'm sorry,” Fisher said.

“I survived,” she wrote, “and there are other women who are now able to hopefully receive justice.”

Hadley had dated Fisher when she was seventeen and he was in his thirties.她最初谎报了自己的年龄，这段关系最终以尖酸刻薄的方式结束。 Now his apparent sympathy didn't last.在他发出第一封信一个月后，他又发出了一封更令人震惊的信。 “I've been asked to provide anything I still have from when we dated,” he wrote. “What I have from that time is a backup of my phone documenting everything you said about a ton of people.”

The people asking for Fisher's phone were allies of the Tates. In the previous month, the brothers' team had devised a strategy to push for Hadley's “full surrender.”他们的律师威胁说，如果她不撤回证词，就要起诉她的家人，赔偿三亿美元。当这没有效果时，他们派了私家侦探去寻找抹黑她的信息。 Some were former FBI agents, others fellow-influencers—including a British man named Sulaiman Ahmed, who had volunteered to travel to Florida.

Petrescu told me that she was dismayed by the decision to send Ahmed. “他傻得像石头一样，”她说。 “一切都是用大锤完成的。” He turned up at Hadley's family home and tried to talk to her parents, then found his way to Fisher. (Ahmed disputes this account.)

When Tate's team learned that Fisher had alerted Hadley to the visit, they were furious, calling him a “loser” and a “highly volatile asset.” Now they had to contend with Hadley's lawyer, who wrote to Fisher, urging him not to participate in a “conspiracy to silence” her client, and pointing out that he had slept with Hadley when she was a minor. He turned over the phone data anyway.

Meanwhile, private detectives probed Hadley's vulnerabilities as a witness.他们发现，在她十五岁时，她多次被五十七岁的瑜伽老师强奸，后者后来因猥亵她和另一个孩子而入狱。在她十几岁的时候，她与年长的男人陷入了一系列令人担忧的关系，在寻求他们的感情时，她多次谎报自己的年龄，并编造了一些关于自己生活的奇幻故事。 All of it went into the dossier.

Tate's most bellicose advocate was Joseph McBride, a lawyer who'd made his name defending several January 6th rioters.麦克布赖德是MAGA圈子里的明星，他在海湖庄园用餐，并与小唐纳德·特朗普成为朋友。他在棕榈滩县对哈德莉提起诽谤诉讼，称她是一个连环骗子和“一个长期以摧毁男人生活为目的引诱男人发生性关系的掠夺者”。 Another one of the Tates' lawyers filed a claim on Fisher's behalf, accusing Hadley of entrapping him.潘科夫和安赫尔通过同一位律师向她提出了索赔。 The Tates also sued Hadley's parents and the family friend who'd initiated the raid.

As the case progressed, McBride reached for a startling variety of arguments to discredit Hadley's claims about the Tates. He accused her of playing “on age-old stereotypes” about “men of African American descent,” saying that such allegations “may have been effective weapons in courts of segregation, but they have no place in today's world.” He maintained that Hadley must have known that Tristan liked “rough sex,” and said complaining that he had choked her was “like getting into the boxing ring with Mike Tyson . . . and being surprised when you get punched.”

To publicize the case, McBride appeared on “Fresh and Fit,” a popular podcast in the manosphere.他称哈德利是“纯粹的邪恶”，并说她从十二岁起就用性来“引诱男人”十年了。 “一个人正在服刑，他不应该在监狱里服刑，”他声称。 “另一个人可能因为与她的互动而自杀了。” If the case went to trial, he said, “I will rip her to shreds . . . body bag, for sure.”或者，他说，她可以撤销她的证词，“整个事情就会停止。” (McBride denies engaging in witness intimidation.)

McBride published Hadley's name, birth date, and passport number, along with her parents' address.然后艾哈迈德分享了多年前她的前男友自杀身亡后她发给费舍尔的一段心烦意乱的语音留言。这篇帖子的标题是“揭露针对毫无戒心的男人的女人”。坎迪斯·欧文斯 (Candace Owens) 在她的节目中专门用了一集来揭秘有关哈德利的说法。麦克布莱德安排塔克·卡尔森飞往罗马尼亚接受泰特美术馆的阿谀奉承的采访，这次采访吸引了 1.12 亿次观看。 McBride later said in a private chat that it had taken him “2 years of relentless work to shift the J6 narrative,” but that he was already “75% there on completely shifting American thought on Tate.”

In the next few months, posts attacking Hadley went viral on social media, and her accounts were deluged with threats—“I'm getting a hitman,” “You deserve to die.” The Tates' advisers warned them not to publicly take part in the smears. Instead, they proposed a “pussy” strategy: letting the affiliate network “have fun with the subject” while the brothers maintained a plausible distance.

Hadley was forced into hiding.她在一份法庭文件中写道：“我在罗马尼亚期间目睹和经历的虐待行为令人心痛。但我因配合执法部门而受到的骚扰和威胁更加严重。” She added, “I am constantly fearful, sad, and alone.”

With the Tates' webcam business dismantled and the travel ban holding them in Romania, they were struggling to fund their life style and pay their legal bills.他们永远无法承认这一点——他们的品牌是建立在炫耀财富的基础上的——但是，安德鲁向顾问透露，“似乎没有人明白我们没有钱。”如果兄弟俩不再去制作视频和宣传自己，他们的在线学校的订阅量就会下降。 “内容已经过时了，”他们的表弟卢克·莱拉斯说。 “我们需要释放安德鲁和特里斯坦。”

In 2024, as the US election approached, Tate pinned his hopes on Donald Trump. Barron Trump had been tasked with reaching a young male audience, and Tate intended to help him deliver. Justin Waller, a close associate who has called himself the Tates' “third brother,” agreed to be the intermediary.

In April, Waller secured an invitation to Mar-a-Lago and began courting Barron. He offered dating advice and introduced him to a tailor, who made the suit that Barron wore to his father's second Inauguration. During the fitting, Waller later said, Barron spoke with Tate over Zoom.

That summer, after the assassination attempt on Trump in Pennsylvania, Tate said that he had spoken to Barron again. “I'm very close with the Trump family,” he said. He and Donald Trump, Jr., also appeared on a live stream to promote a MAGA meme coin. “You've got people attacking you, as far as I'm concerned,” Trump, Jr., said.

Tate spent the day of the election live-streaming, ordering his followers to vote. “America is over if Trump loses,” he tweeted. When the results came in, Petrescu said, the Tates were overjoyed: “Oh, the celebration! Trump is now President. We're going to be fine.”

Afterward, J. D. Vance followed the brothers on X. Then Donald Trump selected one of their lawyers, Paul Ingrassia, to serve as the White House liaison to the Department of Justice. Ingrassia, a twenty-nine-year-old associate at the McBride Law Firm, had worked on the case against Hadley. He had previously tweeted that Tate was “the embodiment of the ancient ideal of excellence,” which was why he had been targeted by “the satanic elite that attempt to systematically program and oppress all men.” The President's counsellor Alina Habba appeared on a podcast with Tate, telling him that she was a “big fan” and declaring, “I've got your back.” She said, “I think you go through a lot of the same 'Show me the person, I'll find the crime' that President Trump has gone through.”

A few weeks after the election, the brothers received more good news: their Romanian indictment had been sent back to prosecutors. The Bucharest Court of Appeal had identified several procedural errors that needed fixing, which meant that the case would be seriously delayed.

The travel ban was still in place. But Rareș Stan had been replaced by a less experienced lead prosecutor, Cezar Profira, whom the Tates and their Romanian lawyers apparently considered a softer touch. (Profira did not respond to requests for comment.) The brothers told me that Profira had joked with them about Hadley during an interview, saying, “You should have bought her a handbag.” In private messages, their advisers talked about needing Profira “on our side.”

Three weeks after Trump's victory, Romania held its own Presidential election, and again the Tates played an unusual part. The brothers had been backing a right-wing candidate named Călin Georgescu, who was a virtual unknown until he won the first round of voting. Georgescu was stridently pro-Russia, and the Kremlin celebrated his victory—but Romania's Constitutional Court annulled the result, citing evidence of interference.

The ensuing controversy gave US officials a pretext for taking an interest in Romania's internal affairs. J. D. Vance, Elon Musk, and other right-wing figures suggested that liberal élites had stolen the election. Trump's special Presidential envoy Richard Grenell accused Romania of joining a global conspiracy to silence “people and politicians who weren't woke.”

Petrescu told me that the team had been lobbying Grenell to help the Tates, and that he was willing. “里克支持孩子们，”她说。 Soon after the cancelled election, Grenell met at Mar-a-Lago with a Romanian politician named Victor Ponta, who later told the Times that they had discussed the Tates' case. (Ponta told me that he no longer remembered this and had no involvement with the Tates. Grenell denied aiding the Tates or speaking about their case with Ponta, though he has publicly affirmed, “I support the Tate brothers.”)

Then, Petrescu told me, the Tates' team devised a PR gambit.慕尼黑安全会议即将召开。如果格勒内尔能够与罗马尼亚外交部长埃米尔·胡雷泽亚努讨论此案，他们将确保一名记者能够偷听谈话内容。 When Grenell met the minister, Petrescu recalled, “Ric only asked, 'Hey, how are the Tate boys?' That's all they needed. It went into a media frenzy immediately. ' The Americans are pressuring for the release of the Tate brothers!' ”

Meanwhile, Vance went onstage at the conference and denounced Romania for annulling Georgescu's victory. He argued that there were “entrenched interests, hiding behind ugly, Soviet-era words like 'misinformation' and 'disinformation,' who simply don't like the idea that somebody with an alternative viewpoint might express a different opinion.”

Hurezeanu, the foreign minister, told me that the government “took this criticism by Vance very seriously,” not least because Romania borders Ukraine and relies on the US for its defense. He said that, when he saw Grenell at the conference, he raised the Tates' case preëmptively. It seemed clear to him that Trump's people were backing the Tates, because of the “electoral support” that they'd provided in 2024;现在兄弟俩似乎正在两国“双边关系中发挥作用”。 (The White House denies this.)

After the conference, Petrescu said, prosecutors reached out to the team and asked, “OK, what do the Americans want?”答案是兄弟俩必须获得旅行许可。 “第二天早上，限制就被取消了，”彼得雷斯库说。一位罗马尼亚高级执法消息人士告诉我，这一决定似乎是“司法系统之外”做出的，并补充说，“被指控贩运儿童的个人能够自由旅行是不正常的。” The longtime Trump ally Roger Stone tweeted, “Richard Grenell secured the release of the Tates,” then hastily deleted the post.

A source who was present when the Tates learned that they were free to travel told me that the message was delivered by a senior Romanian politician—one who had recently been to Mar-a-Lago. The politician turned up at their compound at night in a black SUV “The Americans are asking questions now,” he told them. “Here's your passports back. Happy flying.”

2025 年 2 月 27 日，塔茨夫妇从罗马尼亚飞往佛罗里达州。当他们的私人飞机飞越大西洋时，数千名粉丝对其进行追踪。当飞机降落在劳德代尔堡时，一群记者和围观者聚集在行政航站楼外观看兄弟俩的抵达。 “他们期待着这次盛大的巡演——烈士归来，一位言论自由的英雄，”佩特雷斯库告诉我。 There was a hitch, though: border officials seized the Tates' phones. They were working on behalf of the Department of Homeland Security, which had quietly opened an investigation into the brothers under the Biden Administration.

Tate made a furious appearance on Candace Owens's show. “你认为我睡觉时带着装满证据的手机吗？”他说。 “You think I don't wipe my phone every night? You think I'm dumb? Come get me.”佩特雷斯库告诉我，“我从来没有见过他如此精神错乱。”她建议兄弟俩不要飞往哈德利和她的家人居住的佛罗里达州，她感觉自己得到了严厉的平反。在彼得雷斯库看来，这就像公然的证人恐吓。 Only a couple weeks earlier, Hadley's pro-bono lawyer had filed a counterclaim against the brothers for human trafficking, coercion, defamation, and harassment, alleging that they were attempting to silence her.

Tate embarked on what amounted to a publicity tour of right-wing America.他和特里斯坦被拍到与罗杰·斯通一起抽雪茄，安德鲁称罗杰·斯通为“法律界的老大”。 In Las Vegas, they were greeted ringside by Dana White, the president of the UFC, who hugged them and declared, “Welcome to the States, boys!,” before greeting other guests, including Grenell and Kash Patel, the FBI director.在洛杉矶，泰特会见了坎耶·韦斯特。 He tweeted at California's governor, “I'm in LA. Please begin criminal charges.”

Later, he headed to the Beverly Hills Hotel to spend the night with a model named Brianna Stern.一年前，他认识了斯特恩，当时她来罗马尼亚拍摄照片，宣传他的模因币“$Daddy”。 Tate's seduction followed his familiar pattern: he said that he loved her and called her a whore, demanded that she have his baby and told her, “I want to beat the fuck out of you.” At the hotel, Stern alleges, Tate choked her and battered her head and face, telling her repeatedly that he would kill her if she ever crossed him.

Afterward, she went to a doctor, who diagnosed post-concussion syndrome.斯特恩等待泰特离开该国，然后告诉比佛利山庄警方，他袭击了她。 She also filed a civil suit, attaching her medical report and a series of texts in which he'd threatened to beat her.

When Petrescu learned of Stern's allegation, her reaction was “You gotta be fucking kidding me.” She said to colleagues, “We all have kinks. But can he please, please, please have some vanilla sex for six months?” Petrescu told me she was predisposed to accept Tate's explanation that Stern was an opportunist looking for money. But the texts attached to the lawsuit made for grim reading. “There's stuff I know about the Tates, from reading a lot of the depositions, declarations, text messages, that one doesn't really want to know about their client, ” she said. As a PR person, she added, “you don't question—you can't.” But she began to worry that she was normalizing Tate's behavior: “I started wondering, Is that seeping into my subconscious? Am I becoming that?”

The Tates were in constant motion in the following months, posting from pricey hotels in the Bahamas, Turkey, Kazakhstan, and Hong Kong. Still, Andrew seemed bored. “I miss human trafficking,” he tweeted. “The emotions ran high. Been chasing dopamine ever since.” His online rants were becoming increasingly extreme. He responded to the release of the Epstein files by posting that “women have become such lying whores that it's impossible to take any of these unsubstantiated claims seriously.” Despite his mixed heritage, he promoted the great-replacement theory, declaring, “The west is collapsing because it's full of disgusting traitors. White people who beg to be erased.”

In the American courts, Tate was enjoying a run of good fortune. The LA district attorney announced in June that he would not be prosecuted for his alleged assault on Stern, citing insufficient evidence. McBride claimed that he'd given the DA a “do-not-prosecute packet” demolishing the case; he promptly filed a fifty-million-dollar defamation suit against Stern. In Florida, Judge G. Joseph Curley dismissed most of Hadley's counterclaim, including the allegations that the brothers had harassed her and attempted to coerce her into sex work; he, too, cited a lack of evidence. “Thank God for US justice,” Tate tweeted.

There was a threat from the UK, though. British police had been investigating the origins of the Tates' webcam business, and had charged them with a combined twenty-one counts of rape and trafficking. But a Romanian judge ruled that the brothers could not be extradited until the local proceedings were complete—and, two years after Cezar Profira took over, the trafficking case seemed to be going nowhere. Prosecutors hadn't even fixed the procedural errors in the initial indictment. In chats with advisers, Tristan acknowledged that he and Andrew were effectively being “protected by Romania.”

Romania has recently been rocked by a judicial-corruption scandal: a documentary called “Captured Justice” revealed that judges were being pressured to dismiss or delay politically sensitive cases. More than nine hundred judges and prosecutors have since signed an open letter warning of systemic abuses, and protesters have marched through Bucharest, demanding reform. One of the country's most senior prosecutors called Romania a “paradise for criminals.”

This April, the Tates were released from all bail conditions relating to the Romanian indictment. The brothers were jubilant. “Case never made it past trial. Innocent,” Tate wrote to advisers. “Yeah we spin it as this. If we can,” Tristan replied. “Then when we're reindicted they look stupid.”

Their lawyer responded with three sweating-laughing emojis. “Noooo,” he wrote. “Remember, we need cezar on our side.”

“Cezars a piece of shit I hope he dies,” Tristan wrote. “Me being nice to him has done me no favours.”

In March, Tate addressed the British government in a video posted from a harborside balcony in Hong Kong. “Come extradite me if you have such a strong case,” he said. “You know you have nothing. Everyone else has tried. Everyone else has failed.” He added, “I am the main character of Earth.”

In the political landscape that Tate helped create, women hold an increasingly precarious position. These days, the far-right podcaster Nick Fuentes will openly say that women should be kept in “breeding gulags,” and the ultra-popular Joe Rogan will host a just-asking-questions discussion about whether the feminist revolution was a huge mistake. Influential Christian leaders are calling to repeal women's suffrage. A man was elected President after being held liable for sexual assault.

Even in this milieu, Tate's violent rhetoric stands out. “I saw a woman crossing the road today but I just kept my foot down,” he posted after Trump's reëlection. “Right of way? You no longer have rights.” His influence is being felt around the world. Last year, a seventeen-year-old boy in New Jersey allegedly drove his car into two teen-age girls, killing them both, after joking on a live stream about women getting raped by Tate. In Britain, a man who had recently watched about ten of Tate's videos raped his ex-girlfriend and murdered her, her mother, and her sister using a crossbow. British police have warned that Tate's teachings are radicalizing young men in a “terrifying” manner and declared that violence against women is a national emergency. Reports of rapes and sexual assaults in British schools—some involving victims as young as five—have soared in the past five years, and education leaders describe violent misogyny spreading on an “epidemic scale.” Similar concerns have been reported across North America and Asia.

Yet Tate could have faced justice more than a decade ago, when Navarro, Walker, and Price accused him of assault, if British police had taken their allegations seriously. “They failed to protect the initial victims and created this problem,” the senior Romanian law-enforcement source told me.

In my reporting, I discovered something that the British police had hidden for years: their officers bungled the investigation from the start, making it almost impossible to bring Tate to trial. When Price first came forward, officers neglected to take a statement and discouraged her from pressing charges, telling her that it would be a long and difficult process. They also failed to make a digital recording of Walker's initial statement, instead jotting her words down on paper so sloppily that Tate was at one point referred to as “Michael.” This introduced discrepancies that undermined an account she later gave on video. Multiple items of evidence were also stored incorrectly—including Tate's phone, which was left lying around the station while the case stalled.

When the phone was eventually searched, it apparently contained important evidence—Tate later said that officers had found proof of twelve “bad things I did”—but the chain-of-custody lapse made it practically useless. Prosecutors, hoping to salvage the case, gave police a list of leads, but they failed to act on them. A longtime Hertfordshire detective blamed the malfeasance on “overworked, overstretched” officers who failed to take the women's allegations seriously, partly because two of them were sex workers. “It was one of many cases that were completely cocked up because of poor policing,” he told me.

This spring, after I reached out to current and former officers about the investigation, the Independent Office for Police Conduct suddenly announced that it had launched an inquiry into gross misconduct in the case. The next day, the Hertfordshire police declared that they were reopening their own investigation, more than a decade after the women first came forward.

特里斯坦惊慌失措。 “新的指控决定=新的搜查令，”他写信给他的顾问。 “Means we're not protected by romania at all and he can be picked up at anytime from anyplace.” In a voice note, he added, “We're just gonna have to fucking stay in Dubai and the United States and stop traveling and fucking get some extradition lawyers.”

Navarro, Walker, and Price were already pursuing a civil claim for damages. After the brothers were arrested in Romania, the journalists Jamie Tahsin and Matt Shea reported on the women's stories for Vice News, and another woman said that she, too, had been strangled and raped by Tate in 2014. The four women launched a crowdfunding campaign to “Bring Andrew Tate to Justice in the UK” and filed a claim against him in Britain's High Court.

The Tates were dismissive of the civil case. Andrew wrote to his advisers, “Nobody cares besides 50 retards,” adding, “I'm too important to give them relevance.”但今年春天，当泰特检查众筹活动时，他震惊地发现众筹活动已筹集了超过三十万英镑。 “去找他，”一位女士在十英镑的承诺下写道。另一位捐赠了五英镑，写道：“你的勇敢令人震惊。我们都与你同在。” Looking at the total, Tate wrote, “That's a lot of money.” Then he snapped back into character: “I don't acknowledge my cases. . . . I ignore all this none of it's real.”

Soon afterward, Petrescu quietly resigned. “我离开不是因为他是一个厌恶女性的人。我离开是因为这场悲剧已经变得太令人畏惧，无法观看，”她在计划写一本关于为兄弟俩工作的书的提案中写道。 “They lie like there's no tomorrow,” she told me. “I stopped believing in Andrew.”

When the British civil case goes to trial, one of the witnesses will be Lauren Southern, the former far-right activist, who has since disavowed many of her past views. She told me that she'd decided to get involved after watching Tate's other victims fight for justice. “我有证据，我有可以添加到这些案件中的医疗记录，但我什么也没做，这真的让我心烦意乱，”她说。去年，她自行出版了一本回忆录，详细介绍了她与泰特美术馆的经历。 She broke into tears as she told me that speaking out was “the only way to actually heal anything.”

Tate has always relied on Hruskova to support his story. Since the brothers' webcam operation was dismantled, she has been living in Dubai, where she has trained as a gemologist, specializing in lab-grown diamonds. She declined to speak to me, and has remained publicly loyal to Tate, but there are signs that her perspective may be shifting.

Hruskova recently launched a Telegram channel, Viviens Wonderland, which she describes as a “safe space” for women to discuss topics including recovering from traumatic relationships, sexual assaults, and leaving the sex industry.我今年一月通过公共链接加入了该频道。在里面，赫鲁斯科娃以姐妹般的能量主持聊天，提供安慰、鼓舞士气的谈话和人际关系技巧。

当成员询问她的过去时，她感到愤怒。 “如果你们中任何人提到安德鲁或发表有关他或我们过去关系的言论，你们就会被踢出去，”她曾经宣称。但有时她会放松警惕。 When one member asked her how it had felt to be in a relationship with someone who slept with other women, she replied, “Horrible, do not recommend it to ANYONE ,” and added, “No woman can stomach it for long period of time.”她写道，泰特是她唯一在一起过的男人，自从离开他后，她就决定独身。 It had taken seven sessions of tattoo removal to erase his branding from her skin.

When a group member wrote about missing an ex-partner who had abused her, Hruskova's response was fiery. “你到底想念什么？”她写道。 “How you felt literally worthless? How youd cry with him every day? And then knowing how TERRIBLE you felt and he made you feel, add this—he KNEW what it would cause you and still did it. Thats not love, never was.”

One afternoon in Bucharest, Tristan met me at a surf-and-turf restaurant overlooking a lake. He wore a wide-lapelled burgundy suit and purple alligator winklepickers, made by King Charles's shoemaker.餐桌上，他摘下一块金劳力士，请我感受一下它的重量。 He ordered grilled octopus, followed by two steaks.

Tristan told me that he was determined to clear his name. “I'm Tristan Tate, the human trafficker—that's what everyone says about me. Everyone thinks I'm a horrible person. Everyone thinks I commit crimes against women. Before my daughters grow up, I need to prove that I'm not that guy,” he said. “我的生活已经被毁了，这不应该是我的故事。” He denied using calculated seduction to recruit women, but acknowledged that he'd had sex with many of his workers. “I've got beautiful, naked women in my house,” he said. “What do you think is going to happen?”

The whole webcam business was a distant memory, he insisted. “我学到了很多关于男人的本性，他们是多么变态和怪异，”他说。 “It made me like men a lot less. It certainly made me hate pornography and understand that it's a toxic influence.”当我问他自己有让女人窒息的倾向时，他嗤之以鼻。 “ 'Fifty Shades of Grey' is the most popular female book ever,” he said. “The idea that this book sold hundreds of millions of copies, and that there aren't women who will ask a tall millionaire who likes to wear suits to choke them during sex like it's my idea, is a wild leap of faith.”

After lunch, Tristan beckoned for the bill and refused to let me pay—“I'm a misogynist. You're not allowed.”外面，一名保镖把我带到了一辆正在等候的阿斯顿·马丁面前，特里斯坦把车开到了街上。当他飞驰穿过城镇时，他告诉我，他在罗马尼亚保留着一些高层关系，他们对此案“非常抱歉”。我们把车停在大卫杜夫雪茄商场外。在里面，特里斯坦轻快地走过前台，喊道：“给我一份大的，给她一份小的，”然后带我走进电梯。 When the doors closed, he quipped, “Now you can't leave.”再往上几层楼，门打开，映入眼帘的是一间光线昏暗的休息室，里面挂着厚重的绿色窗帘和黑色皮革扶手椅。

一边抽雪茄，特里斯坦一边提到了来自佛罗里达州的年轻女子埃拉·哈德利。 “她彻底改变了我的人生轨迹，”他说。 “我应该被称为一名运动员、一个生活干净、勤奋、勤奋的商人。” When he thought of Hadley, he said, his mind was “clouded by the fog of war.”

他希望我采访哈德利以及她的无偿律师。 “我希望你能与这些弱智取得联系，”他说。 “我超级希望他们能和你谈谈，因为我会用你的文章在法庭上从他们的屁股里赚更多的钱。” He also urged me to include a “tribute” to Hadley's ex-boyfriend who died by suicide. （哈德利从未接受过媒体采访，拒绝接受采访。）

The elevator door opened, and Andrew Tate walked in. “The bad guy, the bogeyman,” he said, grinning, as he shook my hand.他穿着休闲，穿着绿色麂皮夹克和黑色运动衫，但他的手表上镶满了钻石，脖子上的项链也是如此。 “扼杀音乐，”他告诉特里斯坦。 “I'm low on time, so let's go.”

Tate started by telling me that his reputation as a misogynist was “completely unfair.” He explained, “What I am is a realist who has certain world views on how men should act, which is with honor and duty, and they shouldn't be hurting women in any way. Of course, on the internet, you use hyperbole, and you exaggerate sometimes for comedic effect.”

他告诉我，针对他的案件是他的政敌炮制的。 “What they're trying to do is to attach heinous allegations to my name so that people talk about the allegations instead of my ideas,” he said. “The Western world's on the edge of collapse, and they're very afraid of that.” Both brothers dismissed the War Room messages as “unmoderated chats,” and claimed that the thousands of incriminating texts filed in court had all been mistranslated from English to Romanian.

当我问及旅行禁令是如何取消时，泰特否认唐纳德·特朗普亲自干预，但表示他从布加勒斯特的有权势人物那里得知，这些限制是在美国压力下取消的。 “罗马尼亚刚刚推翻了选举。太多人在关注我们的流程，”他说有人告诉他。 “It's too messy. They want you gone.”现在他确信这个案子会失败。 “The chance of this getting to trial is zero,” he said.

泰特用老生常谈的论点转移了我的大部分问题，但当我问及赫鲁斯科娃时，他似乎措手不及。 “If I'd ever hurt her, why has she released all those videos defending me?”他说。很快，他宣布他需要离开。他在出去的时候碰到了我的摄影师，但拒绝留下来拍照。 “I didn't like some of her questions,” he said.

哥哥走了后，特里斯坦坐在金色聚光灯下的扶手椅上，摆出明显满足的姿势拍照。 “超模吧？”他一边说，一边吸着雪茄。然后他突然想到一个念头。 “You know who ruined the 'I don't need to rape women because I'm handsome' excuse?”他问道。 “特德·邦迪。” He went on, “Ted Bundy ruined it for fucking everyone, because he was a psychopath and he killed a bunch of women and he was handsome and charming. If it wasn't for him, that would still be a plausible reason.”

拍摄结束后，特里斯坦走向电梯。然后他突然回头。 “来吧，抱抱我。”他一边说，一边大步朝我走来。在他双臂环抱我的那一刻，我似乎别无选择。 ❖

item.title: “埃德蒙妮娅·刘易斯：用石头说”评论：一位被忽视的黑人雕塑家
item.description:
2026-06-08T10:00:00.000Z

1859 年秋天，当雕塑家爱德华·布拉克特 (Edward Brackett) 抵达弗吉尼亚州查尔斯敦的监狱时，他发现这位美国最臭名昭著的人的状况并不理想。约翰·布朗受了重伤。在突袭哈珀斯费里的联邦军火库时，他被剑刺伤，头部被砸碎，现在他在牢房里度过了最后的日子，大部分时间平躺在床上，生活在自己的恶臭中。受两名废奴主义者的委托，布拉克特在布朗被绞死前拍摄了他的肖像，制作了一座大理石半身像，将他的脸变成了摩西和苏格拉底的交叉体，布朗标志性的胡子像白色的火焰一样从他的下巴上荡漾开来。 “你看到的半身像有点诗意，”布拉克特在解释他的愿景时写道。 “约翰·布朗本人并不是一个伟人，而是伟大事物的先驱。他是一个盲目的工具，盲目地切断了导致数千人死亡和新时代诞生的道路。”

这也可能是对十九世纪下半叶美国雕塑的辩护，美国雕塑迷失在如此疯狂的内战纪念碑、纪念馆、牌匾和半身像中，以至于你很难找到一个看起来不像某种信条或事业工具的人的雕塑。没有厨房工人在洗锅，也没有闲聊者喝朗姆潘趣酒；大多数人物似乎完全无法提供指示或讲笑话，更不用说跑步、出汗或让血液在血管中流动。它们是严格理想的。即使是这一时期历史上最精明、最自然主义的雕塑之一，奥古斯都·圣高登斯的《纪念罗伯特·古尔德·肖和马萨诸塞州第五十军团》（1884年），也有一个天使从上空俯冲而下，仿佛上帝，而不是人类的决定，正在引导士兵们走向死亡。

1863 年，一位名叫埃德蒙尼亚·刘易斯 (Edmonia Lewis) 的年轻女子看到了布拉克特的布朗半身像，并带着废奴主义者威廉·劳埃德·加里森 (William Lloyd Garrison) 的介绍信，前往布拉克特位于波士顿的工作室学习，当时内战的势头正在向联邦倾斜。刘易斯拿着木槌和凿子站在那里，对布拉克特来说一定像是一颗奇怪的彗星：她身高四英尺，握手坚定，口音独特，而且是密西沙加血统的黑人女性。她的学习速度也很快。首先，她学会了用粘土制作手脚模型，然后她又制作了约翰·布朗的脸章和罗伯特·古尔德·肖的大理石半身像（废奴主义者圈子里的热门商品）。几年之内，她创作的新古典主义雕塑赢得了国际赞誉，最终吸引了弗雷德里克·道格拉斯、尤利西斯·S·格兰特、亨利·沃兹沃斯·朗费罗和教皇庇护九世的注意。但她对艺术的热情总是受到警告和怀疑的影响。一些支持刘易斯的白人废奴主义者会说，她的双手“没有受过足够的教育，无法在大理石上工作”，她最好还是坚持使用灰泥或木头。她并没有忽视她的媒介的讽刺意味：她掌握了一个文明的审美理想，这个文明决心破坏（如果不是摧毁）她所代表的一切。

刘易斯的复兴目前正在如火如荼地进行，一项雄心勃勃的调查始于马萨诸塞州塞勒姆的皮博迪埃塞克斯博物馆，现在正前往佐治亚艺术博物馆。由杰弗里·里士满-莫尔和肖尼亚·L·哈里斯策划的“埃德蒙妮娅·刘易斯：石头里说”汇集了刘易斯生活和工作中零散的片段，汇集了自克尔斯滕·派·别克的“火之子：玛丽·埃德蒙妮亚·刘易斯和艺术史黑人和印第安人主题问题”（2010）以来最大的学术成果。六月还出版了一本书，《移动的石头：关于埃德蒙尼亚刘易斯的艺术》，作者是詹妮弗·德维尔·布罗迪，其中有自己灵巧的档案研究。解开刘易斯艺术之谜并不容易。为了让大家了解学者们所面对的情况：她最著名的作品《克利奥帕特拉之死》重达近三吨大理石，在 1876 年的百年纪念博览会上从罗马运到费城，然后在下一个世纪进行了阿甘正传式的旅程，重新出现在芝加哥的一家酒馆中，然后出现在赛马的坟墓上，然后出现在垃圾场中，直到 19 世纪 80 年代才在郊区地带的储藏室中被发现。商场。这又是刘易斯最著名的作品。

总之，这项新研究对我们对十九世纪美国雕塑的理解提出了严峻的挑战。尽管刘易斯创作了许多标准的新古典主义作品——上流社会的半身像、教堂委托、葬礼纪念碑——但她作品的精明之处在于，它将大理石雕塑的修辞扭曲到个人和政治的新目的。她被誉为多项第一：第一个成为专业雕塑家的非裔美国人；第一位享有国际声誉的非裔美国和土著女艺术家。但展览和奖学金虽然偶尔有点阿谀奉承，但却表明了为什么刘易斯的艺术价值并不取决于这些区别。如果说有什么不同的话，那就是他们被用来抹黑她。亨利·詹姆斯曾将刘易斯描述为一位女性，“她的颜色与她的塑料材料形成鲜明对比，是她名声的辩护者”，仿佛每块大理石都宣布了她的种族，以恐吓你喜欢它。他甚至费心去看她的作品吗？

展览以刘易斯的雕塑“永远自由”（1866-67）拉开帷幕。它大约三英尺半高，展示了一个刚刚听到《解放奴隶宣言》消息的男人和女人。男人赤裸着上身，穿着九分裤，一手举起破碎的脚镣，另一只手搭在跪着的女人的肩膀上，她的眼睛望着天堂。透过内战及其后果的镜头来看，这部作品颠覆了所有的期望。刘易斯的客户大多是白人，他们本希望她让林肯成为自由的执行者，就像托马斯·鲍尔在国会山的“解放纪念碑”（1876年）中所做的那样，但相反，她让她的奴隶们解放了自己。这看起来似乎很有力量，但真的是这样吗？女人显得萎缩、顺从；两个人物的面部特征被淡化，使他们在种族上变得模糊。然后，还有大理石本身：一种古老的媒介，应用于仅仅三年前的历史事件，一种充满纯洁和种族卫生联想的媒介，带有解放的基调。 （如果你考虑到亨利克·易卜生 1874 年的观点，这是一个大胆的决定：“我宁愿看到用黑色雕刻的黑人头像，也不愿看到用白色大理石雕刻的头像。一般来说，这种风格必须符合表现形式的理想程度。”）总而言之，刘易斯的作品充满了矛盾。它是永恒的、令人振奋的新的、回归的、前沿的。这是我见过的最保守激进的雕塑。

刘易斯于 1865 年抵达罗马后创作了《永远自由》。在整个 19 世纪，美国雕塑家纷纷涌入这座永恒之城，以更接近源头——丰富的古物，以及北部的阿普安阿尔卑斯山及其大理石采石场。刘易斯在格里高利安娜街 (Via Gregoriana) 找到了一个空间，这里是新古典主义雕塑界的杰出人物安东尼奥·卡诺瓦 (Antonio Canova) 的前工作室，距离卢多维西别墅 (Villa Ludovisi) 仅一箭之遥，这里收藏着令人印象深刻的古董雕像，其中包括“卢多维西高卢”(Ludovisi Gaul)：这是一个希腊化的雕塑，描绘的是一个刚刚杀死了妻子并将一把剑插入自己胸口的男人，这可能是《永远自由》构图的参考。尽管刘易斯在罗马似乎过着充满活力的生活——举办晚宴、为朋友弹吉他、观看歌剧——但她的情况与其他艺术家不同。她告诉《泰晤士报》 ，她离开美国的原因是“为了寻找一种社会氛围，让我不会经常想起自己的肤色。自由的土地上容不下有色人种雕塑家。”

从童年起，刘易斯的生活似乎就在不幸与幸运、一个人的残酷与另一个人的善良之间摇摆。 1844 年，她出生于纽约州北部，父亲是自由的加勒比黑人，母亲是密西沙加人，十岁之前她就成了孤儿。她被姨妈收留，学习如何制作纪念品，例如串珠莫卡辛鞋和桦树皮篮子，然后出售给尼亚加拉大瀑布周围的游客。她童年的细节并不完整，她可能想保持这种状态：“我的早年生活不会让你感兴趣，”她告诉采访者。 “一切进展顺利，没有发生任何重要的事情，直到我萌生了成为一名雕塑家的想法。”在她同父异母的弟弟塞缪尔的经济支持下，刘易斯得以在纽约中央学院和美国第一所男女同校、种族融合的欧柏林学院接受私立文科教育。塞缪尔似乎是从马克·吐温的故事中走出来的——他过着各种各样的生活，做过走钢丝的人、理发师、金矿工人、花招表演者和房地产开发商。最终，这并没有多大区别。刘易斯被指控毒害两名白人女室友，并遭到白人暴徒的袭击，并在法庭上被无罪释放，但随后又被指控盗窃美术用品，并被禁止重新注册。幸运的是，她在路过俄亥俄州时遇到了弗雷德里克·道格拉斯。他赞扬了她学生的绘画作品，并鼓励她“寻求东方”。 1863 年，刘易斯搬到波士顿，开始跟随布拉克特当学徒。两年后，她来到了罗马。

展览中有刘易斯创作的 29 件雕塑——最早的是 1864 年的，最新的是 1880 年的——尽管她在 18 世纪 90 年代就收到了委托，但她现存的大部分作品都属于她职业生涯的前 20 年。她的富有废奴主义者和名人（例如林肯和朗费罗）的半身像被认为是商业艺术实践的主要内容，但她的集体雕塑却非常棘手。每一个都像一个具有政治自我意识的俄罗斯套娃，一个核心的意义隐藏在下一个核心的意义之中。它们是伪装成装饰性媚俗的激进主义艺术作品，还是相反？它们的设计是为了迎合进步的情感还是激怒他们？在展览中的各种群雕中，包括《老印第安造箭者和他的女儿》（1866-67）、《海华沙的婚姻》（1866-70）和《印第安人的战斗》（1868），我不断回顾和碰撞的作品是《哥伦布》（1865-67）。

波德莱尔在一篇题为《为什么雕塑很无聊》的文章中指出，雕塑的缺点之一是它“同时展示太多的表面”，这与绘画不同，绘画可以提供单一且连贯的观点。当一个人在雕塑周围漫步时，视角会倍增。解释的产生和消解；阴影在表面上发挥作用，使解释场变得复杂，使头发看起来像意大利面条或鼻孔看起来很生气。当然，波德莱尔的论点的有趣之处在于，它很好地解释了为什么雕塑并不无聊。在物体周围自由移动，在静止的金属或石头上精神地绣上一个故事——这就是使形式变得有趣的原因。在“哥伦布”的例子中，这一点对我来说变得很清楚，特别是关于他的脸和他的阴茎。

雕塑展示了这位意大利探险家在一名跪在他身边的裸体土著妇女身后英勇地举起一条毯子。看来他要给她穿衣服，带她进入文明的怀抱。她的手按在胸前，做出感激的姿势，仿佛感谢他殖民了她的土地。 （她很可能是美国的寓言。）展览目录提醒我们，在十九世纪，哥伦布并没有被视为种族灭绝的代理人，而是解放的象征，并认为这件作品“在人物对基督教进步的共同奉献中是和平的，甚至是温柔的”——刘易斯将这位土著妇女变成了美国繁荣的积极推动者。对我来说，这似乎是一个奇怪的读物。其一，哥伦布的脸上带着刘易斯作品中最傲慢、最自信的表情。他有一种自鸣得意、略带邪恶的魔术师的气质：拱起的眉毛和嘴唇上挂着轻蔑的微笑，他的斗篷像塔达一样华丽地介绍他的新收获，一卷看起来鬼鬼祟祟的瑞士羊皮纸从他的左袖子里滑出来。与《永远自由》不同的是，他脚下的男人和女人之间没有一丝温暖。她只是他自尊心的牺牲品。

从一个小角度（向左或向右）观察哥伦布，您会注意到他的腹股沟上方有一个非常小的织物旋钮。与其说是隆起，不如说是勃起的暗示。我最初认为这是他斗篷上的一个偶然的褶皱，但后来陪我去看演出的一位朋友以及其他一些参观者对这个突起进行了评论。也许是凿子无意中敲击造成的；也许勃起根本不存在。但它很重要，因为它巧妙地戳破了英雄神话：现在是性兴奋的颤抖推动了旧世界和新世界之间的文明相遇。花招和走钢丝是刘易斯哥哥的才能，但我越来越相信它们也是她的才能：平衡她自己的政治承诺和赞助人的政治承诺，创造出一种假装说一套做一套的商品。

《哥伦布》、《海华沙的婚姻》和《造箭者》的标准路线是刘易斯使用大理石雕塑使土著主题人性化，以“对抗有害的刻板印象”。后两首作品借用了朗费罗的诗《海华沙之歌》（1855 年）中的场景，这是一部虚构的美国史诗，改编自阿尼西纳贝诗人 Bamewawagezhikaquay（简·约翰斯顿·斯库克拉夫特）的作品的误译。朗费罗的书出版两年内就卖出了五万册，刘易斯认识到将雕塑与流行故事挂钩的商业吸引力。打破刻板印象的地方在于刘易斯对场景和细节的选择。 《造箭者》展示了一位名叫明尼哈哈的达科他州妇女坐在她父亲旁边，一边用灯心草编织垫子，而父亲则在制作箭头； 《海华沙的婚姻》展示了明尼哈哈与奥吉布瓦战士海华沙握手的场景，这是两个交战部落之间的亲密时刻。刘易斯并没有塑造一个轰动的暴力移除场景，而是选择了平静的场景。她还非常注重配饰：熊牙项链、碧玉箭头、柔软的鹿皮。

尽管如此，认为刘易斯的日常和声和材料细节反映了某种激进的艺术计划的论点似乎有些牵强。刘易斯参加了波士顿的黑人活动人士圈子，并向支持妇女权利的宗教组织捐赠了雕塑，但她的作品也可以轻松地适应现状。例如，《印度战斗》讲述的是三名半裸男子殊死搏斗的漩涡，其中一人试图撕下另一人的头皮。这件作品被藏在皮博迪·埃塞克斯展览旁边的一个壁龛里，仿佛这是展览不想支持的反驳论点。刘易斯很可能知道土著暴力是她可以指望的一笔交易。从材料本身到运输成本，大理石工作的成本极其昂贵，她花了数年时间努力维持收支平衡。 “你知道，”她说，“如果我们想活下去，就必须卖掉我们的作品。”

刘易斯于 1907 年在伦敦去世，被埋葬在一个没有标记的坟墓中。 “艺术”这个词通常看起来如此宽大和广阔，但在思考她的作品时，不知何故开始感觉不够充分。刘易斯的雕塑要取得成功，光有艺术还不够；还需要更多的努力。他们被期望更多地证明她的人性或集体进步的纪念碑。即使在今天，她仍因“永远自由”等纪念歌曲而被人们铭记，尽管我有些想知道她最持久的作品是否是装饰音。在皮博迪·埃塞克斯，我不断地回顾《格里特和安·史密斯的紧握之手》（1872 年），这是一幅两只真人大小的手的结婚肖像。格里特·史密斯（Gerrit Smith）是资助布朗袭击哈珀渡口的秘密六人组之一，他和妻子安在纽约的家中经营着地下铁路的一个车站。你可以看出这双手已经握得很近很多年了。既不握紧也不抓握；他们彼此相拥，自然地被他们的拇指锁住。这是一种现实主义，足以在不言明的情况下提出一个理想。史密斯夫妇是著名的活动家，因此这座雕塑可以作为废奴主义事业的工具，一个象征性的协议。但这也是一种简单的爱的表达。 ❖

item.title: 巴里·布利特的《Out Cold》
item.description:
2026-06-08T10:00:00.000Z

在 2026 年 6 月 15 日刊的封面上，艺术家巴里·布利特 (Barry Blitt) 将注意力转向了唐纳德·特朗普 (Donald Trump) 的最新奇观。为了纪念他的八十岁生日以及美国建国 250 周年，总统将在白宫草坪上举办一场 UFC 比赛。 “人们对在那里举行的冲突感到非常惊讶，”布利特说。 “但是，在这样一个充满争议的时刻，也许这是有道理的——以一种令人厌恶、令人厌恶、可耻、残暴的方式。”

有关巴里·布利特 (Barry Blitt) 的更多封面，请参阅下文：

在Condé Nast Store查找巴里·布利特 (Barry Blitt) 的封面、漫画等。
